policy_statement: Chile will close all coal-fired power generation plants nationwide no later than 2040, with closures continuing through 2024 and beyond.
verbatim_text: “Their commitment is materializing itself in specific results, such as the closure of coal-fired power generation plants. The process has already started in 3 plants, will continue with 7 more by 2024, and will conclude no later than 2040 with the closure of all coal plants nationwide.”
section_header: CHILE'S NATIONALLY DETERMINED CONTRIBUTION (NDC) UPDATE 2020 (Foreword message by the President of Chile)
extraction_rationale: Concrete phase-out action with a clear deadline (2040) and interim milestone (by 2024).
reasoning: The verbatim text describes an auditable, outcome-focused commitment: closing coal-fired power plants. It includes a quantified interim milestone (7 more plants by 2024) and a final nationwide endpoint (“closure of all coal plants” by no later than 2040). The language is strong (“will continue,” “will conclude”) and the spatial scope is explicit (“nationwide”). While it does not explicitly cite a legal instrument, the commitment is still measurable and time-bound.
validation_reasoning:
AUDITABLE COMMITMENT: The deliverable is an outcome (plant closures), not a study/process: “closure of coal-fired power generation plants.”
TIMELINE: Explicit deadlines are provided: “will continue with 7 more by 2024” and “will conclude no later than 2040.”
QUANTIFIABLE TARGET: Measurable outcomes are stated: “started in 3 plants,” “7 more by 2024,” and “closure of all coal plants.”
SPATIAL SPECIFICITY: The scope is stated as “nationwide.”
BINDING MECHANISM: No ordinance/law/regulation is cited in the excerpt; it is presented as a commitment, so binding mechanism cannot be confirmed.
LANGUAGE STRENGTH: Strong, directive phrasing with clear outcomes and dates: “will continue…by 2024” and “will conclude no later than 2040.” No weak verbs like encourage/explore appear.
